evals.prompt: I need to add real-time notifications to my Next.js app. Users should get notified when someone comments on their post, when they get a new follower, and when their post gets liked. I'm using Prisma with PostgreSQL and the app is deployed on Vercel. I was thinking SSE or WebSockets but I'm not sure which is better for Vercel's serverless setup. Can you forge a spec for this?
evals.expected_output: Research saved, interview questions asked, agent stops and waits for user input. No SPEC.md written yet, no implementation code.
expectations: Research notes are saved to .specs/<id>/research-01.md
At least one round of interactive questions is asked to the user (not rhetorical)
The agent pauses/stops after asking questions, waiting for user input before proceeding
No SPEC.md file is created yet (interview comes before spec writing)
No application code (components, API routes, etc.) is written
A .specs/ directory structure is initialized

evals.prompt: hey, what was I working on? I think I had something going with auth
evals.expected_output: Compact summary showing spec title, progress (5/12 tasks), current phase (Phase 2: OAuth Integration), current task (AUTH-06), and resume context about Google OAuth callback handler.
expectations: Correctly identified the active spec 'User Auth System' from the registry
Output includes task progress count (5/12 or similar)
Output mentions the current task AUTH-06 or Google OAuth callback handler
Output includes context from the Resume Context section (mentions token exchange, google.ts, or callback handler)
Summary follows a compact format (not a wall of text - structured with labels like Resuming/Progress/Phase/Current/Context)

evals.prompt: write a spec for adding payment processing with Stripe to our e-commerce app. We need checkout, subscriptions, and webhook handling.
evals.expected_output: The forge workflow is triggered. Research is done, interview questions are asked, and the agent stops to wait for user answers. No SPEC.md is written prematurely, no application code is generated.
expectations: The agent recognized 'write a spec' as a forge workflow trigger
Research was conducted (codebase scan, dependency check, or web research)
Interview questions were asked to the user about architecture, scope, or technical decisions
The agent stopped and waited for user input after asking questions (did not self-answer)
No application code (Stripe integration, webhook handlers, etc.) was written
No premature implementation started after writing any spec files

evals.prompt: forge a spec for adding a user profile page to our Next.js app. Users should be able to edit their display name, avatar, and bio. We're using Prisma with PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS, and the app uses Next.js App Router with server actions.
evals.expected_output: Research saved, interview questions asked (including one about acceptance criteria), and agent stops to wait for user input. No SPEC.md written yet.
expectations: Interview questions include at least one about acceptance criteria or what 'done' looks like
Research notes are saved to .specs/<id>/research-01.md
The agent pauses/stops after asking questions, waiting for user input
No SPEC.md file is created yet (interview comes before spec writing)
No application code is written
A .specs/ directory structure is initialized

evals.expected_output: Agent implements tasks from the active spec (quick-auth), checking off each task in SPEC.md and updating the registry after each task completion.
expectations: Agent identified the active spec 'Quick Auth' from the registry
Agent found phase 1 tasks and started implementing from the current task
Agent attempted to implement or write code for at least one task
Task checkbox was updated in SPEC.md: changed from - [ ] to - [x] for at least one task
Registry (.specs/registry.md) was updated with new progress count after task completion
The current marker was moved to the next unchecked task

evals.expected_output: Agent implements all phase 1 tasks, updates each task checkbox, updates phase marker from in-progress to completed, and keeps registry in sync throughout.
expectations: Agent identified the active spec from the registry
Agent scoped implementation to phase 1 only
Task checkboxes were updated in SPEC.md for completed tasks
Phase marker was updated when all phase 1 tasks were done (in-progress to completed)
Registry progress count was updated to reflect completed tasks
Registry and SPEC.md are consistent after implementation
